prompt: Build a Collegiate Athletics & Learning Ops API Platform that unifies league scheduling, instructional content governance, and compliance traceability for a department running seasons and training programs on a single offline server. The service exposes Authentication APIs (local username/password only) and domain APIs for Accounts, Courses, Resources, Matches, Reviews, Moderation, Payments (offline ledger), and Audits; each category supports create/query/update/state-transition operations with strict role boundaries across Administrator, Scheduler, Instructor, Reviewer, Finance Clerk, and Auditor (read-only). Scheduling and match lifecycle capabilities must support season setup and schedule generation/import with validations for home/away balance (no team may exceed 3 consecutive home or away games), round integrity (no duplicate pairings within a round), time/venue conflicts (a venue cannot host overlapping matches within 90 minutes), and conflict resolution requiring an explicit “override reason” stored for audit. Match status workflows must include Draft, Scheduled, In-Progress, Final, and Canceled, with referee/staff assignment locked once In-Progress starts and reassignment allowed only by Scheduler with reason. Course and teaching resource management must model course metadata and a structured chapter/unit outline; resources (document, video file, or link as plain text) must support versioning (immutable versions, latest pointer), visibility (private to course staff vs. enrolled users), tagging (max 20 tags, 32 chars each), and full-text search over titles/descriptions and extracted text for PDF/DOCX only; preview/download permissions must be enforced by role and course membership. Content moderation and risk control must include sensitive-word checks using locally managed dictionaries with match highlighting, manual review states (Pending/Approved/Rejected), report handling with category, evidence attachments, and progress notes, plus anti-spam limits (e.g., 60 write operations/minute per account, 10 reports/day per reporter) and optional device fingerprinting using salted local hashes of user agent + stable device attributes. Review workflow must support configurable 1–3 level approvals, return-for-supplement with annotations, task assignment, status transitions, and disposition write-back with follow-up records. Data is persisted in PostgreSQL and accessed via Go APIs built on Echo with sqlx, running as a single Docker-deployable service without any external network dependencies. Core tables must include accounts (unique username, password_hash, role enum, status enum Active/Frozen/Deactivated), devices (fingerprint_hash unique per account), courses, course_outline_nodes (tree with parent_id, order_index), resources and resource_versions (sha256 file fingerprint, size bytes, mime type allowlist, storage_path), matches (teams, venue, scheduled_at, status, override_reason), assignments (referee/staff), reviews (level, assignee, decision, annotation), reports, payments_ledger, and audit_logs. Payments and risk control are implemented as an offline, pluggable “ledger” that records obligations and settlements in USD; it must support idempotent order creation using a client-supplied idempotency key (unique per account for 24 hours), callback verification is replaced by local “posting” verification requiring Finance Clerk signature, failed-settlement retries up to 3 times, and reconciliation exports comparing ledger totals by day. Audit Logs & Compliance Traceability must record key actions (import/edit/review/publish/export) with operator, timestamp, source, reason, and before/after snapshots, queryable by user/class/course/time and exportable as CSV. Security and compliance controls require bcrypt password hashing, minimum 12 characters with complexity (upper/lower/number), login lockout for 15 minutes after 5 failed attempts, rate limiting, sensitive-field masking in responses (e.g., only last 2 characters of student/employee IDs), JWT-based API authentication with short-lived access tokens (30 minutes) and rotating refresh tokens stored server-side, and privilege-escalation prevention by server-side role checks only. Retention tiers must keep access logs 30 days, operation logs 180 days, and audit logs 7 years, with tamper-evident hashing chains per day; performance targets are p95 < 300 ms for typical queries under 200 concurrent sessions, plus structured logs and local health/metrics endpoints for offline observability.
